title: my finger around.
content:
my finger around.
I watched and I waited. Finger on the trigger. Snapping in time lapse the conquest of my desires. Apollo, my mighty master and future lover, pistol in hand, held firmly against the glass, perpetrating our future love. Every moment I captured, tiny eye watching and waiting behind the glass cage of the camera -- every memory captured lost in the present and preserved for the past. Never mind the pesky crow nevermore, nor Pallas and her unjust unkind ungrateful ways. These are my services rendered unto Apollo and my rendered reward with be just kind grateful, I know of it.
I watched and waited. Then waited no more. Nevermore. The crimson tide splashed over her naked breast, and she did not move nor twitch nor make a sound, but her crooked smile remained mocking me even as she entered into death. And into death I felt the pangs of regret spread from my core outwards. Loved she was, now mourned she is -- by me and the rest of the world that had to lose such a perfect specimen of beauty. I held her in our final lovers embrace, her head lolled about her neck, and pressing her against my body I felt a hardness swell and a cry from within. There was life still in her, it was our life -- a fusion of our love -- and it screamed to be released. I released it so just to find it mocked me as well, the child just as dead as the mother -- my son.
context: From document: The Raven and The Crow (contd.) — chunk 2 (chunk 2/2)
